title: Guided tester session
subtitle: Operator notebook — for you, not the demo audience
northStar: See what BTC options imply, where you disagree, and what payoff fits — in under 15 seconds.

steps.title: Open the demo
steps.context: You are running a **guided research walkthrough**, not a sales pitch. The product explains curves and disagreement; your job is to keep the room calm and collect honest reactions.
steps.goal: Confirm the demo loads on the big screen before traders arrive.
do: Open the demo URL on the laptop (screen share ready).
Optional: Refresh priced inputs (Deribit) once so you are not waiting live.

steps.title: Set expectations
steps.context: Traders fear hot tips and black boxes. Lead with **not advice** and **structure legibility**.
steps.goal: They know this is exploration + feedback, not a trade recommendation.
do: Face the room, not the keyboard.
Keep the chart visible if already loaded.
steps.say: This is a BTC options research cockpit — not advice. I'll show what the market prices in for an expiry, where a view might differ, and how payoff fits. About five minutes, then I want your honest reaction.
steps.skip: How to use this demo expander on the site — only if they seem lost before the lab.

steps.title: Load live quotes
steps.context: Deribit marks power the orange market-implied curve. Without refresh, the lab may look empty or stale.
steps.goal: Live data is on screen so the chart is trustworthy.
do: Wait for completion (don't narrate errors — retry or use pre-refreshed session).
steps.say: I'm pulling live BTC option quotes from Deribit for this run.

steps.title: Anchor on implied lab
steps.context: The **Bitcoin implied lab** is the wedge: one expiry, one chart, market-implied shape first.
steps.goal: They see the main object — the priced distribution — without scrolling confusion.
do: Scroll to Bitcoin implied lab — market-implied view as the anchor.
Pick an Expiry that loaded cleanly.
steps.say: This chart is built from today's option prices. Purple and orange show what shape the market has priced in for this expiry — a readout, not a forecast of what will happen.
steps.skip: Distribution stats table above — only if they ask for numbers.

steps.title: Overlay a belief
steps.context: Your belief is **optional** and always shown **against** the market curve, not replacing it.
steps.goal: They see that the tool separates market pricing from personal view.
do: Click one belief preset (or adjust belief controls on the left).
Point at how the overlay changes on the chart.
steps.say: If I think differently — higher, wider, whatever — I state that here. It's my view on the same chart, not the market's.
steps.skip: MVP1 output line (watch / candidate) — mention only if it reads clearly; no edge claims.

steps.title: Read disagreement
steps.context: Disagreement is **descriptive** — where views diverge — not a buy/sell signal.
steps.goal: They understand the second core insight after the chart.
do: Open Belief vs market — at a glance (or Review disagreement digest).
steps.say: This names where my view and the market's priced view differ — diagnostic, not a trade signal.
steps.skip: Strategy families, trade ticket, Verification — unless they pull you there.

steps.title: Freeze / full app (optional)
steps.context: On the **public demo**, freeze history may be limited. Full app = discipline: capture what you saw before outcomes.
steps.goal: They see there's a review loop, not just a one-off chart.
do: If on demo: mention Get full access / research beta CTA.
If on full app: briefly show freeze under implied lab.
steps.say: The full app lets you freeze what the system showed so you can review later without hindsight bias. That's the discipline piece.
steps.skip: Skip entirely if time is short or demo has no CTA configured.

steps.title: Comprehension check
steps.context: This is your **validation signal** — pass if they name chart / implied / disagreement.
steps.goal: Learn if the UI communicated the main object (not whether they like you).
do: Stop demoing. Look at them, not the screen.
steps.ask: What was the main thing on screen — in your words?

steps.title: Return intent
steps.context: Stronger than compliments: would they **use** it before trading?
steps.goal: Honest pull signal for wedge proof.
steps.ask: Would you open this before your next BTC options trade?
steps.skip: Paid interest / pricing — session 4+ only, not first meetings.

steps.title: Log the session
steps.context: No row = session didn't count for validation. Two minutes now beats guilt later.
steps.goal: One row in reality checks so the scoreboard and morning report move.
do: Note: profile, comprehension Y/N, return Y/N, one-line surprise or confusion.
Add row to VALIDATION_REALITY_CHECKS.md § MSOS P8 tester metrics.
steps.say: Thanks — this is exactly the feedback I needed.

panic.title: Panic reset
lines: Breathe. You are the guide, not the quant.
Point at the screen. Let the chart talk.
You don't derive the math — Verification is the audit trail.
Ask them a question — takes pressure off you.
panic.mathDeflect: Live Deribit chain into a density shape. Orange from the chain, purple is a simpler baseline. Trust strip and Verification show provenance — I don't derive it by hand on a call.

survey.title: Session feedback survey
survey.subtitle: Log validation metrics — saved to MSOS web feedback + copy row for reality checks.
